category_title: Students who exhibit physical distress and avoidance behaviors (crying, falling to floor) in loud environments
minimal_context: The student is walking with the teacher and an aid through the gross motor room.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the ball in the gross motor area.

STUDENT: (Bounces on the ball, makes high pitch noises and flaps hands.)

TUTOR: Okay, it is time to walk with me and the aid. Give me your hand.

STUDENT: (Holds the teacher's hand and starts to walk.)

TUTOR: Keep walking with us.

STUDENT: (Walks with the teacher, occasionally crying.)

options.A: Use a request for clarification in the gross motor room, asking the student for explanations and elaborations about why they are currently crying.
options.B: Develop a plan for the gross motor room: identify the walking path, label the ball, and ask the student how they will walk.
options.C: Stop walking with the student and the aid, and wait in the gross motor room until the student's crying ceases.
options.D: Think aloud about metacognition and cognitive strategies when examining the student's actions, comparing their current crying to the expected walking behavior.
